headline: Systemd
description: systemd is the init system and service manager for most Linux distributions (since ~2015). It manages services, sockets, timers, mounts, and devices via unit files. `systemctl start/stop/enable/disable service`. systemd unified Linux service management across distributions, replacing SysV init and Upstart.

citation.name: systemd: Modern Linux System Management (2025 Edition)
citation.author: multiple
